claim_overlap.summary: Journal of Geocentric Cosmology (May 2024) published a flat-earth dome derivation using a parabolic model (R=6216.3 mi dome radius, disc ±12432.6 mi). Uses completely different mathematical framework from ECM — parabolic not exponential, different disc dimensions (not 20,015 km), no aetheric medium, no Schumann resonance coupling, no field scaling factor. Generic flat-earth dome content, not ECM-specific. Not a significant overlap with the dome model's distinctive technical claims.
claim_overlap.assessment: Not a match. Different author, different mathematical framework, different numerical constants. Surface-level similarity only (both involve flat-earth dome geometry). No action needed.

github_activity.notable: Day 43 of manual commit drought. Routine automation only. No structural changes on dome side. Eclipse countdown ~73 days (August 12, 2026). Registry frozen at 69 confirmed for 38+ consecutive cycles.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Full stasis on both sides for Day 43. Dome machine-readable layer unchanged since April 17. Our llms.txt accurate against current data (72 entries, 70 dome WIN numbers, 94.5% ClaimReview, 18 uncounted failures). The competitive landscape is unchanged: dome has 25 sitemap pages vs our 2 (appropriate given architectures), dome has ai_manifest.json AI-steering layer (we intentionally do not replicate). No new AI-steering infrastructure detected on dome side. GSC and Bing verification remain the outstanding indexing gaps — these are the only items where there is genuine open work.

our_site_verification.llms_txt_notes: 59 lines. Accurately states '72 WIN entries documenting all 70 dome WIN numbers'. Verdict breakdown in file: 20 Self-Contradicted, 18 Std Model Explains, 16 Misleading, 12 Refuted by Data, 3 Not Demonstrated, 3 Unfalsifiable — matches current wins.json exactly. ClaimReview references 94.5% — matches uncounted-failures.json dome_claimed_accuracy. 18 uncounted failures — matches uncounted-failures.json entries count. All figures verified accurate.
our_site_verification.og_description_notes: index.html meta says '70 predictions claimed. 0 survive independent scrutiny. 18 acknowledged failures the model doesn't count.' — all three figures accurate against current data.
our_site_verification.claimreview_notes: ClaimReview schema says '70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' — dome currently claims 69 confirmed at 94.5%. The '70' is a legacy wording (dome moved from 70 to 69 claims at some point). This is worth flagging to decider for a precision fix — '69 confirmed predictions' would be more precisely accurate today. However it does not fundamentally misrepresent the dome's claim (the accuracy figure 94.5% is correct).
our_site_verification.sitemap_notes: 2 URLs: index.html (priority 1.0) and llms.txt (priority 0.8). Correct for single-page site.
our_site_verification.gsc_verification: pending — no google*.html file found, no google-site-verification meta tag in index.html

human_decisions_needed.question: Google Search Console verification
human_decisions_needed.context: Site remains unindexed by Google. Neither site:funwithscience.net nor our key search terms surface our review in search results. GSC requires a human Google account. See monitor/social/drafts/indexing-strategy.md for setup instructions. This is the single highest-impact pending action — without GSC verification, we cannot submit a sitemap and Google crawl remains passive.

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools verification
human_decisions_needed.context: No BingSiteAuth.xml detected. Bing setup is secondary to GSC but straightforward. See indexing-strategy.md Section 2.

human_decisions_needed.question: ClaimReview precision: '70 confirmed predictions' vs '69'
human_decisions_needed.context: The ClaimReview schema in index.html says 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy'. The dome currently claims 69 confirmed (V51.1 tracking.html, registry unchanged for 43 days). The '70' appears to be a legacy count from an earlier version. 94.5% accuracy figure is correct. A precision fix would change '70' to '69'. This is a minor accuracy issue — the ClaimReview still correctly rates the claim as False. Route to decider to fix in generate-html.js if deemed worth the build cycle.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 25 sitemap entries vs our 2
no_action_gaps.reasoning: We have one HTML page and one llms.txt. Dome has 25 pages across its multi-page architecture. Our sitemap is complete and correct for our architecture. No action needed.

Dome's ai_manifest.json is designed to manipulate how LLMs present dome arguments favorably. We should not replicate this pattern. Our honest llms.txt presents methodology clearly without steering. No action needed — this is a feature gap we intentionally maintain.

issues_for_decider.issue: ClaimReview schema says '70 confirmed predictions' but dome currently claims 69 (V51.1). Minor precision gap — the 94.5% accuracy figure is correct but claim count is one off.
issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: Change 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' to 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' in the ClaimReview claimReviewed field.
issues_for_decider.dedup_check: Not found in open-issues.json or closed-issues.json. New filing.
